hypothesis: A terminal-zero island may preserve a complete-word phase that reduces the final-tail problem to fixed transducer fibers.
interpretation: The terminal pair determines the next word head. Every zero step deletes one fixed p/u phase letter from the normalized word, and every consecutive zero island remains in that phase. The phase is the normalized ordinary state's bit-length parity.
limitations: does not prove resets occur infinitely often
does not exclude infinite orbits in either fixed terminal fiber
does not prove the alternating inverse lift has infinite support
does not exclude eventual center period two
does not solve Rule 30 center nonperiodicity
proof_scope: The terminal-head identity, zero-step deletion recurrence, fixed-phase theorem, and arithmetic parity interpretation are all-word statements proved in proofs/informal/problem1_period_two_zero_phase.md. Exhaustive campaigns are bounded regression checks.
question: problem1

exact_conclusions.arithmetic_phase: the phase is exactly the parity of the normalized ordinary state's bit length
exact_conclusions.final_tail_reduction: a final zero tail must remain forever in one of two fixed terminal fibers, with one phase letter deleted per block
exact_conclusions.fixed_zero_phase: every consecutive terminal-zero island has one fixed p/u phase
exact_conclusions.remaining_target: exclude infinite actual-fringe-driven orbits in both fixed terminal fibers
exact_conclusions.terminal_head_code: the terminal pair determines the next complete-word head letter

phase_parity.exact_reason: p(0)=3 has bit length 2, u(0)=1 has bit length 1, and every subsequent forward generator raises positive bit length by 2

zero_deletion.exact_deletion: if G=t^ell a B and G(11)=00, then U_q(G)=t^(ell+1) U_q(B)
zero_deletion.phase_propagation: the normalized word after every zero output begins with the same phase a in {p,u}

zero_island_drivers.exact_conclusion: every finite consecutive-zero prefix remains in its initial p/u phase
